sections.paragraph:
fut. יִתּשׁ prop. to pluck up plants (see Hophal); hence-(a) to destroy cities, Psa 9:7 idols, Mic 5:13.-(b) to expel nations from a land (opp. to נָטַע), Deu 29:27 1Ki 14:15 Jer 24:6, וּנְטַעְתִּים וְלֹא אֶתֹּוש “I will plant them, and not pluck them up,” i.e. I will give them settled abodes, and will not expel them; Jer 12:14, 15 Jer 12:15 42:10 45:4 .

Niphal, pass. to be expelled (used of a people), Jer 18:14 31:40 Amo 9:15 to be overthrown, as a kingdom, Dan 11:4.

(2) i.q. נָשַׁת ( Isa 19:5) to dry up, as water, Jer 18:14.

Hophal, to be plucked up, Eze 19:12.
